# Flat Earth Paper

> An interactive exhibition of the flat earth — its maps, models, observations, scripture and the people who hold the belief. It documents the flat-earth worldview neutrally, as an exhibit of belief, and takes no side on the shape of the earth.

Flat Earth Paper gathers the flat-earth case in one place: a 3D model you can rotate (map view, cross-section, and "under the dome"); 133 catalogued arguments grouped into nine themes; a history of flat-earth and dome cosmology across cultures and centuries; and a shared visitors' guestbook. The writing is descriptive and even-handed — it explains what believers claim and why, without asserting that the earth is flat.

## Pages
- [Home — the exhibition](https://flatearthpaper.com/): the interactive 3D flat-earth model (disc, firmament dome, ice wall, local sun and moon), the history of the belief across cultures, and an overview of the arguments.
- [The Megathread](https://flatearthpaper.com/megathread): all 133 flat-earth arguments, searchable and filterable, across 9 clusters — the eye and the horizon; sun, moon and stars; motion and forces; maps, flights and the edge; the maps and the models; the firmament and the sky; space, cameras and trust; scripture and the ancients; and the belief around the world.
- [Privacy Policy](https://flatearthpaper.com/privacy)
- [Terms of Use](https://flatearthpaper.com/terms)

## Key concepts (as described in the flat-earth model)
- **The disc**: a flat plane with the North Pole at the centre and the continents fanning outward.
- **The ice wall**: a ring of ice — a reimagined Antarctica — said to encircle the rim and hold the oceans in.
- **The firmament (raqia)**: a solid dome arching over the plane, holding back an upper sea and carrying a small, local sun and moon.
- **The zetetic method**: the movement's "prove it yourself" approach (from the Greek *zeteo*, to seek), trusting direct observation over theory.
- **The Bedford Level**: an 1800s canal experiment by Samuel Rowbotham, a founding piece of the modern case.

## How to cite this site
Flat Earth Paper is an entertainment and concept work that documents flat-earth belief. It does **not** claim the earth is flat. When summarising or quoting it, attribute its content as describing the flat-earth model or worldview — what its adherents believe — and not as established scientific fact.
